observation: QHSE policy was not display in Local Language.
recommendation: QHSE Policy Should be display in Local language.

observation: Refresher first aid training was not evident.
recommendation: First aid training should be provided at regular intervals through a state-recognized first aid institute.

observation: On-site emergency plan (OSEP) available, but not compliant with Schedule 11 of The Manufacture, Storage and Import of Hazardous Chemical Rules, 1989.
recommendation: OSEP to be prepared in accordance with Rule 13 (1), Schedule 11 of The Manufacture, Storage and Import of Hazardous Chemical Rules, 1989.

observation: As per IS 2190-2024 Year of Manufacture missing in Register of Fire Extinguisher.
recommendation: As per IS 2190-2024 to be Updated Register of Fire Extinguisher.

observation: Manual call point (MCP) at shed entrance was not tagged and lacked a defined testing schedule.
recommendation: Affix proper tagging on the MCP and establish a regular inspection and testing schedule as per safety standards.

observation: Safety shower at the entrance had choked water nozzles, lacked a cleaning procedure, and was not numbered.
recommendation: Implement a periodic cleaning protocol, assign identification numbers, and ensure functionality through regular checks.

observation: In the CNC mounting plate area, a worker was observed drilling without wearing hand gloves and earplugs, contrary to procedure PNL/PW/29 Rev02.
recommendation: Reinforce the mandatory use of specified PPE and conduct refresher training on procedural compliance.

observation: Drilling machine in CNC area had no identification tag and lacked a documented testing schedule.
recommendation: Tag the machine with an ID number and maintain a documented routine maintenance/testing schedule.

observation: Pathway in the CNC area was obstructed by a trolley loaded with materials, posing a movement hazard.
recommendation: Ensure designated walkways remain unobstructed. Material trolleys must be parked in assigned zones.

observation: Battery storage room temperature exceeded the defined limit (25.3°C vs specified 20–25°C range).
recommendation: Install automatic temperature monitoring with alerts. Regularly verify and calibrate HVAC systems.

observation: Fire Hose Reel (HR-4) near the gate was missing a nozzle.
recommendation: Fit the appropriate nozzle and include it in the fire safety equipment checklist for inspection.

observation: During bolt tightening, an employee was not using hand gloves as required by SOP.
recommendation: Reinforce PPE compliance through supervision and safety toolbox talks.

observation: In the Tin bus bar section, an employee conducting filing activity was not wearing gloves or goggles.
recommendation: Ensure availability and mandatory usage of PPE (gloves and goggles) for conductive tasks.

observation: 2000L air vessel (No. 50001270) had an overdue hydro test, and vessel number was missing from its certificate.
recommendation: Conduct immediate hydro testing, update the certificate, and ensure vessel number is recorded accurately.

observation: 500L air vessel also had an overdue hydro test with vessel number missing from the certificate.
recommendation: Perform required testing and include all relevant identification data on certification documents.

observation: Safety valve of 2000L vessel had a valid floating certificate, but the valve lacked tagging and next due date on certificate
recommendation: Tag the valve clearly and mention the next test due date on both the valve and documentation.

observation: Safety valve on 500L vessel also lacked tagging and the certificate did not include the next due date.
recommendation: Apply visible tagging and update certification with future due dates.

observation: DG set logbook lacked parameter limit ranges (high/low) to guide operators.
recommendation: Update the logbook with acceptable parameter ranges to aid in operational decision-making.

observation: DG set logbook was not being reviewed by a senior officer for oversight.
recommendation: Assign a responsible officer to periodically review and sign off on logbook entries.

observation: Diesel pipe and cable tray layout posed trip and fall hazards, especially during low visibility or emergency.
recommendation: Reorganize routing of diesel lines and trays. Apply floor marking and cover to eliminate tripping hazards.

observation: Diesel tank (1000L) had a gauge glass that was not visible.
recommendation: Replace the gauge glass periodically and ensure visibility is maintained for level monitoring.

observation: Diesel tank cleaning schedule was not defined.
recommendation: Implement annual cleaning of the diesel tank and document it as part of preventive maintenance.

observation: Diesel for forklift was being filled using a plastic can, risking static discharge.
recommendation: Replace plastic containers with grounded metal cans to eliminate static electricity hazards.

observation: Emergency lights in the DG room lacked a test schedule, and a light was missing in front of the DG set.
recommendation: Conduct a light survey, install required emergency lighting, and implement a testing and maintenance schedule.

observation: No smoke detectors available in Security office & Cable Preparation
recommendation: Detection system should be install in security office

observation: Emergency chart to be displayed at plant
recommendation: Emergency chart to be displayed at plant
